target: WIN-063 detail_evidence — defense neutralization (defense_survives=4 in WIN-063.c3 advocate_mode). Three targeted insertions in data/wins.json:WIN-063.detail_evidence to harden the response against the dome defender's strongest pivot: (1) the 3:1 ratio is "verifiable from CHAOS-7" but not derived from any of the four dome geometric parameters; (2) the SAA-shoulder mechanism cannot be selected by an axisymmetric H(r) model; (3) the "Olson & Deguen are also fitting" rebuttal conflates fitting-to-independent-inputs with fitting-to-the-output-itself. detail_verdict_text already addresses adoption-vs-derivation; detail_evidence needs the rebuttal-handling text. TLDRs already updated by prior decider patch — no TLDR change.

decision.rationale: Option A (three find/replace patches into existing detail_evidence prose, preserving the c2-era kernel-of-truth framing and the c3 lack-of-derivation framing already integrated by the decider) is correct because: (1) curmudgeon WIN-063.c3 explicitly recommends "minor_edit" not "major_rewrite"; (2) the prior decider integration already inserted the "adopts from CHAOS-7" acknowledgement into both detail_evidence and TLDRs, so the c3 holes 1, 2, and 5 are essentially closed already (verified against current data/wins.json:WIN-063); (3) the surviving defense rated 4 has three distinct weaknesses left to address — the four-parameter list is incomplete (P1), the SAA-longitudinal-arbitrariness gap (P2), and the "you accept fitting too" rebuttal (P3) — each of which can be answered with a single targeted insertion; (4) wholesale rewrite would risk losing the existing kernel-of-truth framing that the dome correctly identifies a real puzzle (asymmetric decay is genuinely measured by CHAOS-7 — Finlay et al. 2020) — that framing is currently embedded in the first paragraph and worth preserving.
decision.why_not_option_b: Option B (rewrite detail_evidence wholesale into a single defender-neutralization treatise) would have produced a more rhetorically unified text but at the cost of: (a) losing the existing c2-era CHAOS-7 / Olson & Deguen / Aubert citation backbone that has already passed two cycles of curmudgeon review; (b) duplicating the lack-of-derivation framing already in the (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) clause structure; (c) requiring re-validation of all five citation links and DOIs that are already in place. The find/replace approach is reversible and patch-auditable; wholesale rewrite is not.
decision.why_kernel_of_truth_matters_here: The dome correctly observes that hemispheric magnetic decay IS asymmetric — that is a real, measured phenomenon documented in CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020 doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9) and not a strawman. The dome's rough qualitative claim that "a perfectly symmetric core flow would produce ~1.0x" is also correct in spirit — a true axisymmetric MHD geodynamo with no boundary heterogeneity would not naturally produce a 3:1 hemispheric ratio without breaking symmetry somewhere. Naming this puzzle is the kernel. The patches preserve that acknowledgment by leaving the first paragraph intact (which credits the puzzle and the CHAOS-7 measurement) and add the rebuttal-handling specifically against the defender's "Olson & Deguen are also fitting" pivot — addressing the survival mechanism without dismissing the underlying observation.

This is more diagnostic than the current generic 'no derivation' complaint." The current text mentions only two of the four. patches.target_path: WIN-063.detail_evidence — sentence about axially-symmetric H(r) (third paragraph fragment beginning "Although the dome's H(r) function...")
patches.uniqueness_verified: Substring count = 1 in current data/wins.json:WIN-063.detail_evidence (verified 2026-05-09 — anchor is the unique "Although the dome's H(r) function is axially symmetric (depending only on radius)" phrase).
patches.find: However, no published formula connects this flow topology to a specific hemispheric decay asymmetry ratio — meaning a dome defender who argues 'our toroidal aetheric vortex naturally produces hemispheric asymmetry' has not addressed the core objection: without a predicted numerical value derived before measurement, any observed asymmetry ratio will retrospectively 'match' an unspecified topology.
patches.replace: However, no published formula connects this flow topology to a specific hemispheric decay asymmetry ratio — meaning a dome defender who argues 'our toroidal aetheric vortex naturally produces hemispheric asymmetry' has not addressed the core objection: without a predicted numerical value derived before measurement, any observed asymmetry ratio will retrospectively 'match' an unspecified topology. The dome's stated mechanism — 'the sub-terrestrial return path exits preferentially through the SAA shoulder' — adds a separate problem: the South Atlantic Anomaly sits at a specific terrestrial longitude band (roughly 30°W to 90°W on the globe), but the dome's H(r) is axisymmetric in longitude and no element of the published dome geometry breaks longitudinal symmetry. Nothing in the four canonical parameters singles out the SAA's longitude over its mirror image (~150°E) or over any other location on the disc. The mechanism therefore names where the asymmetry shows up empirically (the SAA shoulder, observed by CHAOS-7) without explaining why an axisymmetric dome should produce a return-path exit there rather than elsewhere — a post-hoc location label, not a geometric prediction.
patches.rationale: Tracker note (b): "SAA-shoulder mechanism is post-hoc (dome geometry has no torque mechanism that specifically selects the SAA latitude over other longitudes)." This patch makes that explicit and gives a concrete mirror-image counterfactual (~150°E) the dome's axisymmetric H(r) cannot distinguish from ~30°W. This pre-empts the defender pivot to "but the SAA shoulder is the mechanism" — the answer is: naming a location is not a derivation if the geometry has no way to single out that location.

patches.target_path: WIN-063.detail_evidence — end of first paragraph, replacing the parenthetical "(Note: geodynamo simulations use independently measured boundary conditions...)" with a paragraph-break and a dedicated rebuttal-handling paragraph
patches.uniqueness_verified:
Substring count = 1 in current data/wins.json:WIN-063.detail_evidence (verified 2026-05-09 — the parenthetical "(Note: geodynamo simulations use independently measured boundary conditions from seismology, but the hemispheric asymmetry emerges from the physics of fluid convection — it is not fitted to the asymmetry itself.)

" appears exactly once).
patches.find: (Note: geodynamo simulations use independently measured boundary conditions from seismology, but the hemispheric asymmetry emerges from the physics of fluid convection — it is not fitted to the asymmetry itself.)
patches.replace:
(Geodynamo simulations use independently measured boundary conditions from seismology; the asymmetry emerges from the simulated physics, not from the data the simulation is later compared against.)

A dome defender will respond that the geodynamo's reversed-flux patches are themselves fitted to the observed asymmetry rather than derived from a more fundamental theory, so the dome's adoption of 3:1 from CHAOS-7 is methodologically equivalent to mainstream practice. This conflates two different kinds of fitting. Olson & Deguen 2012 and Aubert et al. 2013 build MHD simulations parameterized by independently measured core-mantle-boundary conditions from seismology (PREM/AK135 reference models, mantle-tomography heat-flux maps) — the asymmetry emerges as an output of the simulated fluid dynamics from those independent inputs, and the simulations are then validated against (not tuned to) the CHAOS-7 hemispheric ratio. The dome offers no analogous independent input from which a specifically 3:1 ratio emerges; the 3:1 is read directly off CHAOS-7 and copied into the WIN-063 paragraph as if that act of reading were itself a derivation. Fitting boundary conditions from seismology and letting MHD physics produce the asymmetry is structurally different from naming a ratio after measuring it.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon advocate_mode preemptive_recommendation #3: "Add a sentence to detail_evidence directly addressing the 'you accept Olson & Deguen as fitted-not-derived too' rebuttal." The existing parenthetical "(Note: ...)" gestures at this distinction but is buried inside the first paragraph and reads as an aside rather than a rebuttal. Replacing it with a labeled defender-anticipation paragraph makes the distinction explicit: independent-input MHD ≠ no-input ratio-naming. PREM/AK135 reference and mantle-tomography heat-flux maps are the named independent inputs, both of which are publicly cited in geodynamo literature and which the dome has nothing analogous to. This is the patch most directly aimed at the defense_survives=4 rating.

new_evidence_added: PREM/AK135 reference models (named in P3 as one of the seismology-derived independent inputs to MHD geodynamo simulations).
Mantle-tomography heat-flux maps (named in P3 as the second category of seismology-derived independent input).

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender: "Olson & Deguen 2012 also tunes their reversed-flux patches to fit CHAOS data — your independent-input distinction is contrived."
anticipated_objections.response: Olson & Deguen's reversed-flux patches sit at the core-mantle boundary, whose topology and heat-flux variations are constrained by seismology and mantle tomography (PREM/AK135 + S40RTS-style models) — independent of any geomagnetic measurement. Tuning the patch amplitudes is a calibration of degrees of freedom that have an independent existence; the dome's "3:1 ratio" is itself the only degree of freedom and has no independent existence outside CHAOS-7. There is no analog in the dome model to "we constrained the boundary conditions from seismology and let the fluid dynamics emit the asymmetry."

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender: "The mirror-image counterfactual is unfair — the SAA exists where the data shows it; you can't fault us for naming a real place."
anticipated_objections.response: The point is not that SAA-as-location is wrong; the point is that an axially-symmetric H(r) cannot derive SAA-as-location. A model whose geometry has no longitudinal preference cannot logically predict that the return path "exits preferentially" through one specific longitude band. Naming the empirical location of the asymmetry is a description, not a prediction. The dome would need to break longitudinal symmetry in its geometry — e.g., introduce a non-axisymmetric H(r,φ) — to make SAA-shoulder a derived rather than observed feature.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender: "You list four parameters but disc_radius and firmament_height already appear in our derivations of other quantities (e.g., 1.57c phase velocity in WIN-001). The 3:1 might just be derivable, you simply haven't found the formula."
anticipated_objections.response: The burden of derivation is on the model, not the reviewer. Review of WIN-001 (1.57c) shows the derivation steps explicitly: phase_velocity = c × π/2 ≈ 1.57c, where π/2 comes from the disc_radius-to-firmament-height ratio. No comparable derivation exists anywhere in the dome materials for the 3:1 hemispheric ratio. Until such a derivation is published, "might be derivable" is exactly the unfalsifiable claim that WIN-063 is being faulted for.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender: "The PREM/AK135 reference is overly specialist — readers won't follow it."
anticipated_objections.response: PREM and AK135 are the standard one-dimensional reference models for Earth's seismic velocity structure (Dziewonski & Anderson 1981; Kennett, Engdahl & Buland 1995) and are published in introductory geophysics texts. Their inclusion in P3 anchors the "independent input" claim against actual published data products that any geophysicist can verify. The TLDR remains accessible; the detailed argument stands up to specialist scrutiny.

kernel_of_truth: The dome correctly identifies that hemispheric magnetic decay IS asymmetric — that is a real, measured phenomenon documented in CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020) and not a strawman. The dome's informal claim that "a perfectly symmetric core flow would produce ~1.0x" is correct in spirit: a fully axisymmetric MHD geodynamo with no boundary heterogeneity would not produce a 3:1 hemispheric asymmetry. The puzzle the dome points at — "why is the field decaying asymmetrically?" — is real. What the dome does not do is solve that puzzle. Standard physics solved it via reversed-flux patches and core-mantle-boundary heat-flux variations decades ago, both grounded in independent seismology data; the dome merely renames the cause (toroidal return path, SAA shoulder) and adopts the observed ratio.
test_recommendation: After integration, run `node test.js 2>&1 | grep -E "WIN-063|asymmetry|geodynamo"` to confirm no test breakage. Specifically: (1) detail_evidence length test should still pass (post-patch length will be ~4,400 chars vs current 3,678 — within tolerance); (2) the "American cell" / "Atlantic cell" cross-WIN consistency test should still pass; (3) any test that pins the parenthetical "(Note: ...)" string will now fail — if such a test exists, update it to pin the new defender-anticipation paragraph instead. Manual cross-check: render dashboard and walkthrough, eyeball WIN-063 panel for paragraph flow.
closure_recommendation_for_decider: After integrating P1/P2/P3, mark ISS-1916 status=fixed (not fixed-pending-verification — the patches directly address the c3 advocate_mode defense_survives=4 finding, and the "Olson & Deguen also fit" rebuttal is the highest-rated surviving move per the c3 review). Optionally trigger a c4 cycle on WIN-063 to verify the rated-4 defense no longer survives — but a c4 is not blocking; the c3 review's preemptive_recommendation list is fully addressed by these three patches plus the prior decider integration of detail_verdict_text.

tldr: WIN-063 defense (rated 4): dome says "we DID specify 3:1, you strawmanned us — and Olson & Deguen are also fitting." Three patches: (P1) list all four dome parameters explicitly, none derive 3:1; (P2) SAA-shoulder mechanism cannot be selected by an axisymmetric H(r) — mirror-image (~150°E) is geometrically equivalent; (P3) replace buried parenthetical with a labeled rebuttal — Olson & Deguen tune CMB boundary conditions from seismology, not the asymmetry itself; the dome offers no analogous independent input.
